description_long: This is a vintage tabletop radio receiver with a rectangular wooden cabinet featuring rounded edges and a polished veneer finish. The front panel displays a large speaker grille above a black control area, which includes two rotary knobs, a horizontal frequency indicator, and a row of white rectangular push buttons. The back panel is made of perforated fiberboard for ventilation and access to internal components, with printed technical information and connection ports. The sides and top are smooth, showcasing the wood grain pattern. The overall design is classic and utilitarian, typical of mid-20th-century European radios, and the object appears well-preserved with minimal wear. The radio is labeled with the model name "PODHALE" and other technical markings on the rear.
description: A vintage wooden tabletop radio receiver with a polished veneer, front speaker grille, control knobs, and frequency indicator. The design is classic and well-preserved, with technical markings on the back.
description_view_0: Front view showing the speaker grille, two rotary knobs, frequency indicator, and white push buttons.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the smooth, polished wooden surface with visible wood grain and rounded edges.
description_view_2: Rear view featuring a perforated fiberboard panel with printed technical information and connection ports.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, highlighting the continuous wood grain and rounded cabinet edge.

license_info.creator_display_name: Museum of Engineering and Technology, Krakow
